source_display: Shift Careerspan toward hiring-side data sales
evidence: Pivot to HIRING side: realized we've built a high-signal community (~3-4K users, 1/3+ with multiple stories, deep data from curated alumni associations with zero marketing).
Ivor suggested Handshake unprompted as the perfect acquirer; his incoming Head of Strategic Ops will reach out once she joins next week.

source_display: Set up an employer trial account for Ivor
evidence: V offered to create an employer trial account so Ivor can 'tinker through anything you want' and develop a feel for the product himself.

source_display: Send materials package to Ivor's Strategic Ops hire
evidence: V will send supporting materials (deck, overview, partnership framing) so Ivor's Strategic Ops hire can get up to speed on the Handshake conversation.
Both agreed to catch up next week after Ivor's new Strategic Ops hire settles in.

source_display: Talent scanning is Careerspan's core value
evidence: 'What you said yesterday made me realize ... the only thing that actually matters ... do I have anyone fucking good?'—the quality of the talent pipeline is at the heart of everything.
Ilse insisted applicants must explicitly click submit after seeing an opportunity so the response rate becomes a credible signal; V agreed.

source_display: Use the pre-list as a trust demonstration
evidence: V framed the CSV/pre-list as proof that Careerspan actually produces names worth recruiters' attention, not merely a comfort blanket.

source_display: Require recruiter agreement on candidate usage
evidence: Ilse demanded formal agreements so recruiters cannot add Careerspan candidates to their own mailing lists, and V agreed.

source_display: Delay Holly's silent scans until Monday
evidence: Ilse decided to postpone the Agave and Deep Tune silent scans to Monday so she can rethink the build after the strategic shift.

source_display: Maintain both brand-name and hungry recruiter channels
evidence: Decision to pursue the Dual-Track Recruiter Strategy: brand-name partners like Superposition for credibility while hungry individual recruiters deliver execution.

source_display: Focus high-signal user acquisition in NYC
evidence: The team committed to prioritize New York City for acquisition, leaning on clubs like Harvard Club of NYC, Cornell Club of NYC, AI Circle, and other alumni networks.

source_display: Use every connection for the NYC push
evidence: The team agreed to 'use every single connection' they have for the NYC focus and emphasized high commitment levels.

source_display: Double down on NYC alumni networks
evidence: Logan outlined the NYC channels (Harvard Club of NYC, Cornell Club of NYC, AI Circle, NYC-specific alumni networks) as the levers to lock in high-signal users.

source_display: Brand-name partners provide legitimacy even without users
evidence: V argued Superposition would not bring users but would create a trust cascade and legitimacy signal, while hungry recruiters execute placements.
